items.title: Cody Gakpo breaks down in tears as he scores World Cup goal just days after partner’s heartbreaking miscarriage
items.summary: CODY GAKPO broke down in tears after scoring an emotional goal - just days after his partner's miscarriage. The Dutch striker scored to put the Netherlands ahead on 72 minutes against Morocco in the last 32. The ball fell kindly for the Liverpool forward who fired into the back of the net as the def

items.title: Morocco beat Netherlands in dramatic penalty shootout to set up Canada clash
items.summary: Morocco secured their spot in the World Cup last 16 after beating Netherlands in a dramatic penalty shootout following a 1-1 draw in Monterrey.

items.title: ‘They wouldn’t be champions’ – Jurgen Klopp takes dig at ARSENAL as he rages at VAR over disallowed Germany goal
items.summary: JURGEN KLOPP aimed a dig at Arsenal after Germany’s disastrous World Cup exit. The 59-year-old former Liverpool boss watched his nation tumble out of the tournament on penalties for the first time, against minnows Paraguay. items.title: The end for Nagelsmann? Germany suffer their 'next football nightmare'
items.summary: Germany make a shock last-32 exit against Paraguay, losing a World Cup penalty shootout for the first time to pile pressure on boss Julian Nagelsmann.

items.title: Inside Paraguay's tears of joy over their seismic World Cup shock
items.summary: How Paraguay marked one of the biggest wins in their footballing history with euphoric celebrations.

items.title: 'Germany play one way - and it doesn't work anymore'
items.summary: The team in the studio talk about what went wrong for Germany as they go out of the World Cup to Paraguay

items.title: Holland 1 Morocco 1 (2-3 on pens): African giants WIN on penalties after Verbruggen’s freak World Cup shootout gaffe
items.summary: CODY GAKPO deserved to score - but the Netherlands did not. Morocco deserved to equalise and win - but for Gakpo to end up on the losing side is yet more personal heart-break. He could be excused for an invisible first 70 minutes - most people wouldn't have been strong enough to even get on...

items.title: ITV slammed for World Cup coverage of Holland vs Morocco as fans MISS start of extra-time after blunder
items.summary: ITV missed the start of extra-time of Holland's clash with Morocco. Issa Diop cancelled out Cody Gakpo's emotional opener in injury time. And that sent the tense last-32 showdown for another half an hour. ITV cut to an advert break at the end of normal time. But by the time they came back, the actio

items.title: ‘Extremely bitter’ – Germany icon, 40, immediately retires after shock World Cup penalty shootout exit to Paraguay
items.summary: MANUEL NEUER immediately re-retired from international football after Germany's shock World Cup exit to Paraguay. And he admitted: "It's extremely bitter for it to end like this." Neuer, 40, retired from Germany duty after Euro 2024. But he made a shock U-turn to appear for the DFB at this World Cup

items.title: 😞 Why Messi-Ronaldo showdown now looks unlikely
items.summary: Portugal's draw with Colombia on Saturday means a destined quarterfinal matchup between Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi is off until possibly the final.

items.title: Morocco win shootout to send Netherlands home
items.summary: Morocco beat the Netherlands in a penalty shootout after the match finished 1-1 to advance to the round of 16 on Monday night.

items.title: Paraguay stun Germany on pens after VAR drama
items.summary: Kai Havertz, Nick Woltemade and Jonathan Tah missed crucial penalties as Germany were eliminated from the World Cup in a shootout by Paraguay.

items.title: Nagelsmann: Germany no longer 'first-class team'
items.summary: ulian Nagelsmann said Germany are no longer a "first-class team" after another early World Cup exit, but added he wants to stay on as head coach following their penalty shootout defeat to Paraguay.

items.title: Brazil survive World Cup scare against Japan
items.summary: Casemiro said Brazil avoided one of the all-time great World Cup shocks against Japan by keeping "calm."

items.title: Aguirre: Azteca fans can get Mexico past Ecuador
items.summary: Mexico manager Javier Aguirre said he hopes local fans will make the difference for El Tri when the World Cup co-hosts face Ecuador at Mexico City's Estadio Azteca in the round of 32 on Tuesday in a matchup of two Latin American sides.

items.title: Cáceres: Leaks about Uruguay's Bielsa 'twisted'
items.summary: Uruguay defender Sebastián Cáceres said people twisted the narrative around Marcelo Bielsa to make the manager look bad following their elimination from the FIFA World Cup in the group stage.

items.title: Berhalter in tears after son Sebastian's WC goal
items.summary: Chicago Fire manager Gregg Berhalter was brought to tears when thinking about son Sebastian Berhalter's first World Cup goal for the United States men's national team against Türkiye in the final group stage match.

items.title: Paraguay were more than just 'uncomfortable' for s...
items.summary: Germany expected Paraguay to be "an uncomfortable opponent," but the South American side were so much more: They ended Die Mannschaft's World Cup.

items.title: World Cup VAR review: Why Germany were wrongly den...
items.summary: For the tournament, we are taking a look at the major incidents to examine and explain the process in terms of both VAR protocol and the laws of the game.
